response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad software engineering background with notable geospatial and distributed systems exposure, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust for production routing/navigation pipelines — is not met. His C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a student thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his skills section. His primary language is TypeScript/Node.js, and while his GISCollective work demonstrates genuine geospatial interest, it is focused on data collection and web mapping for NGOs, not high-performance routing tile generation or navigation data pipelines at production scale.
strengths: Sustained geospatial domain interest via GISCollective and Green Map GIS application, showing familiarity with spatial data concepts
Production experience with low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, real-time metadata)
Demonstrated ability to work across the full stack and adopt new technologies, including DLang, Swift, and embedded systems
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience; C++ is absent from the skills section and last touched in 2014–2015 in a limited context
No experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or traffic signal integration — the core technical domain of the role
Geospatial work is primarily web-based data collection for small NGOs, not the scale or latency profile required for global production routing infrastructure
